Tomato Early blight leaf.untreated_note: Spreads upward through canopy. Lower leaves defoliate first, reducing photosynthesis and yield.

Tomato late blight leaf.untreated_note: One of the fastest spreading plant diseases. Can destroy entire crop in 7-10 days in wet conditions.

Tomato leaf bacterial spot.untreated_note: Spreads via rain splash. Accelerates significantly in wet weather.

Tomato leaf mosaic virus.untreated_note: Spreads through contact and aphid vectors. Infected plants never recover — focus on preventing spread.

Tomato leaf yellow virus.untreated_note: Spread depends on whitefly population. Control vectors immediately to slow progression.

Tomato mold leaf.untreated_note: Thrives in high humidity. Reducing moisture slows spread significantly.

Tomato septoria leaf spot.untreated_note: Causes rapid defoliation from bottom up. Can defoliate entire plant within 2-3 weeks.

Tomato two spotted spider mites leaf.untreated_note: Mite populations double every 3-5 days in hot dry conditions. Act within 24 hours.

Potato leaf early blight.untreated_note: Progresses from older to younger leaves. Reduces tuber size if defoliation occurs before maturity.

Potato leaf late blight.untreated_note: Fastest spreading disease in this database. Entire field can be destroyed in under 2 weeks.

Corn leaf blight.untreated_note: Spreads upward through canopy. Critical if it reaches ear leaf before grain fill.

Corn leaf gray spot.untreated_note: Slower progression than blight but causes severe yield loss if upper canopy is affected.

Corn rust leaf.untreated_note: Wind carries spores across entire fields rapidly. Scout neighbouring farms too.

Bell_pepper leaf spot.untreated_note: Spreads to fruit causing scabby lesions that reduce market value significantly.

Cherry Powdery Mildew leaf.untreated_note: Thrives in dry conditions unlike most fungi. Does not need rain to spread.

Grape leaf black rot.untreated_note: Berries are most vulnerable. Fruit infection causes total loss of affected clusters.

Grape powdery mildew leaf.untreated_note: Infected berries split and are unmarketable. Protect clusters before 10mm berry size.

Peach leaf bacterial spot.untreated_note: Rain events dramatically accelerate spread. Each rain = new infection cycle.

Apple Scab Leaf.untreated_note: Primary infection season is spring. Summer infections less severe but still reduce fruit quality.

Apple rust leaf.untreated_note: Requires nearby cedar/juniper as alternate host. Remove alternate hosts to break the cycle.

Strawberry leaf scorch.untreated_note: Reduces runner production and next season's yield. Remove infected leaves before winter.

Squash Powdery mildew leaf.untreated_note: Spreads rapidly in summer heat. Can cover entire plant within 2 weeks if untreated.
